title: Obione acuminata
intro: Obione acuminata, also known as Redstem Filaree or Redstem Stork's-bill, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polygonaceae, characterized by its distinctive red stems and small, white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Obione acuminata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Obione acuminata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, from respiratory problems to skin infections.
taxonomy: Obione acuminata is classified within the family Amaranthaceae. This classification places it within the order Caryophyllales. At the kingdom level, it is classified as Plantae. The species is further classified as a eudicot, within the clade Angiosperms. The classification of Obione acuminata is also reflected in its binomial nomenclature.

Its leaves and roots are used to treat ailments such as fever, digestive problems, and skin conditions. Additionally, the plant is believed to have antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and infection prevention. preparations: Obione acuminata is reported to have been used in traditional herbal preparations for its purported medicinal properties, though I couldn't find any specific information on its use.
